title: Uniface Messages 9000-9999

Uniface Messages 9000-9999

Uniface Messages 9000-9999

Refer to the Uniface message number, message text, and an explanation of the message.

The following table refers to messages with numbering between 9000-9999.

Message number Message text Explanation
9001 Fatal external structure error. Uniface encountered an error in the internal table administration for the component. Ensure that your system is configured properly. Recompile your components and application. If the error occurs again, contact your Uniface representative.
9002 Proc cannot return to original component. An internal error; Uniface is unable to reload a previous component from user workspace. Ensure that your system is configured properly. Recompile your components and application. If the error occurs again, contact your Uniface representative.
9004 Virtual memory page load failure (seek error). Self-explanatory.
9010 Out of memory; cannot claim %%$sub more; total segmented memory claimed %%$sub. Uniface requires more memory than is currently available. Contact your system administrator.
9014 Conceptual structure and database definitions are inconsistent. Self-explanatory.
9015 Database access requested on non-loadable data. Uniface tried to execute an I/O request on an included entity. This is not possible because all I/O must go through the main entity. Correct the definition.
9016 System needs more channels for files. Uniface needs at least one more channel for I/O operations, but all available channels are occupied.

Use, for example, $maxopencursorsora or $maxfiles to enlarge the total number of available channels. If possible, check the operating system or DBMS, or both.
9019 Cannot claim enough dynamic memory from system to execute. Your application requires more memory to run. Contact your system administrator.
9020 Data fetch error; see message frame. An internal error; a Uniface I/O request for continuation segments is not granted by the DBMS.

Check your message frame for the DBMS message issued. Ensure that your authorization and DBMS quotas are sufficient.
9024 Application stopped due to logon error. The message indicates that the application was not able to connect to the database. Either the password was incorrect or the DBMS is not running, and therefore cannot accept a password.
  • Check that your password is correct, or that the DBMS is installed or both.
  • Check that the database server is running.
  • If a database server is running, check that it is the correct database.
9030 Sort data exceeds Uniface sort limitations. Insufficient memory present; cannot sort. The size of the sort key and the number of records to be sorted must not exceed eight megabytes.
9031 Cannot allocate sort space, sorting not done. Insufficient memory present; contact your system administrator and request more memory.
9032 Platform not supported The Windows platform is not supported for this version of Uniface.

This error occurs when:

  • You are running on a Windows platform that is not supported, such as Windows NT or 95.
  • Uniface can't detect the Windows platform because it is a new version of Windows that is not (yet) supported, or a very old or obscure version.
9033 Runtime error: Proc stack overflow There are too many nested calls to ProcScript modules.

This situation can occur when a ProcScript module is called recursively many times. Each call adds a level to the ProcScript call stack.

The maximum number of levels depends on the type of ProcScript module.
9034 Certification checks failed for 'object type' 'object name'. The specified object has not been certified.

Troubleshooting:

  • Make sure the object you are trying to load has been certified.
  • If the object is certified, then the private key used to certify might not match the public key in your ENCRYPTED_PATHS.
  • Make sure all your dependencies are certified. If you do not recognize the specified object, it might be loaded from usys.uar.
9715 Command line format is incorrect. When generating Java files for Java call-in, the required format is:

/sto /lan=jav {/pth = Path} {/pkg = Package} FileName Signature | SignatureList

9800 UDE action "Action" failed; ... The UDE functionality supports compiling, exporting, importing, and converting or copying repository data. It is used by IDE command line switches such as /cpy, /cpt, and so on, and it is available in ProcScript through the $ude function. It is not available to the Uniface runtime (uniface.exe). 
  • 9800 - UDE action "Action" failed; cannot find IDE entity descriptors.
    This can occur if the entity descriptors (in ide.uar) cannot be found.
  • 9800 - UDE action "Action" failed; UDE function is not available at runtime.
    This can occur when using $ude or IDE command line switches with the Uniface runtime.
  • 9800 - UDE action "Action" failed; IDE component UUREPOS not found.
    This can occur if the UUREPOS component (in ide.uar) cannot be found..
Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
